methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found an official course-site final-paper guidelines PDF plus mostly IRIS research PDFs.
Firecrawl search found the official declaration PDF, the same final-paper guidelines PDF, research repository items, a Minerva research page, and a procurement PDF.
Firecrawl search found central news/event pages about AI in university teaching, but these were treated as event/advisory material rather than binding student policy evidence.
Official PDFs were fetched directly after Firecrawl PDF extraction returned only partial page windows for the larger guidelines document.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery did not find a central university-wide Sapienza generative-AI policy page. The usable evidence is limited to two official course-site PDFs for Sapienza CAD Psychology undergraduate final papers: final-paper writing guidelines and the required originality/integrity declaration. Claims are therefore scoped to that process.

stopConditions: Stop after official PDF evidence supports narrow course-scope claim candidates.
Do not infer a university-wide Sapienza policy from CAD Psychology course-site materials.
Do not use search snippets, event announcements, research papers, or project pages as policy evidence.

artifacts.claimText: For Sapienza CAD Psychology undergraduate final papers, generative AI tools such as ChatGPT and Gemini may be used as drafting support only with careful, transparent use and personal critical revision by the student.

artifacts.claimText: For Sapienza CAD Psychology undergraduate final papers, the guidance treats uses such as relying on AI for bibliographic discovery, unverified citations, article summaries, critical analysis, delegated writing, automatic responses to supervisor feedback, data manipulation, fake results, or uncited AI content as illicit.

artifacts.claimText: Sapienza's final-paper originality declaration requires students who used AI, automatic translators, grammar checkers, or data-analysis software to transparently declare the tool function and specific use, and says AI was not used to generate substantial content or automate any section.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Linee guida per la stesura della prova finale triennale
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: L'uso dell'intelligenza artificiale generativa (IA) attraverso piattaforme quali ChatGPT, Gemini e servizi analoghi, può rappresentare un valido supporto durante la stesura, ma richiede un utilizzo attento, consapevole delle sue limitazioni e condotto con la massima trasparenza.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: For CAD Psychology final papers, Sapienza says generative AI can support drafting but requires careful, limitation-aware, transparent use.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Linee guida per la stesura della prova finale triennale
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Sono considerati utilizzi illeciti dell'IA ai fini della redazione dell'elaborato di prova finale i seguenti comportamenti: Cercare articoli e altre fonti bibliografiche; Usare citazioni non verificate; Riassumere articoli; Generare la struttura dell'elaborato o l'indice; Evitare l'analisi critica; Delegare la scrittura; Manipolare dati; Generare risultati falsi; Plagio.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Dichiarazione di Originalità e Integrità Accademica dell'Elaborato Finale
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Qualora siano stati utilizzati strumenti di intelligenza artificiale, traduttori automatici, correttori grammaticali o software per l'analisi di dati, la loro funzione è stata dichiarata in modo trasparente all'interno dell'elaborato, insieme al loro utilizzo specifico; Non ho impiegato strumenti di intelligenza artificiale per generare parti sostanziali del contenuto o per automatizzare la redazione di nessuna delle sezioni dell'elaborato.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The declaration requires transparent disclosure of AI or related tools and states that AI was not used to generate substantial content or automate any section.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Section 3, Utilizzo dell'Intelligenza Artificiale e Strumenti Tecnologici Avanzati

artifacts.title: Sapienza University of Rome AI Policy Staged Crawl
artifacts.summary: Staged official-source crawl for Sapienza University of Rome found no central university-wide generative-AI policy page. It found two official Sapienza course-site PDFs for CAD Psychology undergraduate final papers. Four agent-reviewed claim candidates are limited to that process: transparent support use, listed illicit uses, required originality/integrity declaration, and declaration language for AI-tool disclosure and no substantial AI-generated content.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Only public official Sapienza-domain sources were used; no login-gated, private, CAPTCHA-protected, paywalled, or robots-disallowed sources were accessed.
The approved claims are scoped to CAD Psychology undergraduate final-paper materials and should not be presented as a university-wide Sapienza AI policy.
Event/news pages, research project pages, repository research PDFs, procurement records, and search snippets were rejected or excluded as policy evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
